_comment: DRAFT - starter content, revise me. Code-review rubric used to score a PR before the human gatekeeper checklist (.github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE/gatekeeper.md) is filled out. Weights must sum to 1.0. Each criterion is scored 0-100 by the reviewer/agent; a dimension's score is the average of its criteria scores; the overall score is the weighted sum of dimension scores.

scale.description: Each criterion is scored 0-100. 100 = fully meets the criterion with no notable issues. 0 = criterion is violated or entirely unaddressed.

criteria.description: Admin-only operations (create/update/delete events) are enforced server-side via requireAdmin, not just hidden in the UI.
All user-supplied input is validated and/or parameterized before use; no raw string interpolation into SQL.
Session/auth state is not leaked (e.g. password hashes never returned in API responses; cookies are httpOnly).
No newly introduced dependency with known critical vulnerabilities or unnecessary broad permissions.
Changes respect routes -> services -> data-access layering: routes contain no SQL or business rules, services contain no req/res, data-access contains only SQL.
Changes are scoped to what the issue's Acceptance Criteria require; no unrelated refactors bundled in.
Application code changes do not modify or weaken tests/**, especially tests/golden/.
Errors use the existing error/middleware conventions (e.g. AppError subclasses, central error handler) rather than ad-hoc try/catch with inconsistent responses.
New code matches existing naming, file organization, and formatting conventions in the surrounding module.
Code is reasonably commented where intent is non-obvious; no dead code or leftover debug statements.
New tests follow the // AC-n tagging convention from .factory/qa.nlspec.md and live under the expected tests/ structure.
